rationale: The cited paper appears substantively relevant because both studies center on assortment/search context and postchoice satisfaction, and the citing note explicitly says it extends prior assortment research on externally fixed choice sets—exactly the kind of design used in the cited paper. Given this strong construct overlap (assortment size/search, expectations/disconfirmation vs. satisfaction dynamics) and a confirmed DOI match, this should be kept for full relation judgment rather than dropped as incidental mention.
